relations: Sputnik 1 was launched by the Soviet Union, marking the start of the space age
Yuri Gagarin was launched into space by the Soviet Union aboard Vostok 1
Russia continued the Soviet Union's space exploration efforts after its dissolution
The Soviet Union was a major competitor in the original space race against the United States
Apollo 11, a mission by NASA, marked the first time humans landed on the moon and was a significant achievement for the United States

NASA, the space agency of the United States, is responsible for the country's space exploration efforts. This includes notable missions such as Apollo 11, which marked a significant milestone in space exploration history.
The United States was a major competitor in the original space race and continues to be involved through both NASA and private companies
Yuri Gagarin's historic spaceflight was aboard Vostok 1
NASA initiated the Space Shuttle Program in the 1980s
NASA contributed to the construction and operation of the ISS
Neil Armstrong was an astronaut on the Apollo 11 mission and the first human to set foot on the moon
Buzz Aldrin was an astronaut on the Apollo 11 mission and walked on the moon
Russia, following the Soviet Union's dissolution, continues its involvement in space exploration and the modern space race
The Space Shuttle Program enabled the construction of the ISS
SpaceX has launched multiple crewed missions to the ISS
Elon Musk founded SpaceX and leads its space exploration efforts
SpaceX, as a private company, has entered the modern space race with significant advancements and plans for Mars
Blue Origin has entered the modern space race with its plans to commercialize space travel
